words.meaning: meaning "sickness," may identify a tune for this spiritual lament.

notes: A prayer for help against enemies, Psalm 54 opens with an introductory plea for deliverance and closes with a matching report of deliver ante- The description of the threat of enemy aggression is balanced by the prayer that God will destroy the enemies. The psalm's structurally highlighted center presents the psalmist's statement of trust in God a plea to God for help (54:1-2 [54:3-4]) " "save me, 0 God, by your name (fimka)" b report that the enemies seek to kill the psalmist (54:3 [54:5]) c CENTER: statement of trust: "God is my help!" (54:4 [54:61) b' wish that God would kill the psalmist's enemies (54:5 [54:71) a' report of God's help and vow to praise (54:6-7 [54:8-91) " "I will give thanks to your name (f`imka) . for he has delivered me" (David A. Dorsey, The Literary Structure of the Old Testament)

JST There is none that doeth good, no not one. They were in great fear, for God hath scattered the bones of him that encampeth against him. O Lord, thou hast put to shame those who have said in their hearts there was no fear, because thou hast despised them. Oh that Zion were come, the salvation of Israel; for out of Zion shall they be judged, when God bringeth back the captivity of his people. And Jacob shall rejoice; Israel shall be glad.
This psalm was composed when the Ziphites, men of the same tribe with David, instigated Saul to destroy him, and directed him how to accomplish it, 1 Sam. 23-24.
